prompt: The test has a floating-point precision problem. What is the bug?
choices: Math.sqrt() does not work with irrational numbers
Comparing doubles with exact equality is fragile; use assertEquals with a delta: assertEquals(1.414, result, 0.001)
explanation: Although this particular case may pass (Math.sqrt(2) is specified by IEEE 754), exact floating-point equality is generally fragile. The proper way is assertEquals(expected, actual, delta) with an acceptable epsilon: assertEquals(Math.sqrt(2), result, 1e-10). This is more robust and communicates intent.
